thesis: LandBridge owns and manages 315k+ Delaware Basin surface acres, collecting fee-based revenue from easements, surface royalties, resource sales, and oil and gas royalties. FY2025 consolidated revenue was $199M (+81%); Q1 2026 revenue $51M (+16% YoY) with raised 2026 adjusted EBITDA guide $210–230M. The Up-C structure leaves Class A holders with a minority economic slice (~42% of consolidated net income) while sponsor units retain control and related-party WaterBridge revenue is material.
why_market_wrong: The market may treat LandBridge as a high-growth land compounder after the post-IPO re-rating, or dismiss it as sponsor-controlled fee stream with no asset floor. At ~$78, proof-first component value ~$41/sh sits well below price while Lawrence base owner-cash yield remains near flat. Predictive attribute: IPO / Up-C complexity and digital-infrastructure narrative ahead of contracted SEC economics.

key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share

key_assumptions.source: Q1 2026 filing net debt / economic units
No executed lease economics in SEC packet

open_questions: What enforceable economics accrue to LB from the Alpha Digital 2 GW campus when an executed agreement appears?
How much of current fee growth is recurring volume, price, mix, and one-time activity?
Verify Class A cash attribution vs distributions through Up-C on a trailing-four-quarter basis.
Track digital infrastructure and PowerBridge option contracts in future SEC filings.
